title: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
description: Computer systems that can perform tasks normally requiring human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition, decision-making, and language translation.

title: Model Risk Management (MRM)
description: The framework for identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling risks arising from the use of AI and machine learning models in business decisions.

title: AI Governance Framework
description: A structured approach to managing AI systems throughout their lifecycle, ensuring compliance, ethical use, and effective risk management.

title: Explainable AI (XAI)
description: AI systems designed to provide transparent, interpretable explanations for their decisions and predictions, crucial for regulatory compliance and trust.

title: FCA (Financial Conduct Authority)
description: The UK regulatory body overseeing financial markets and firms, responsible for AI governance standards in UK financial institutions.

title: PRA (Prudential Regulation Authority)
description: UK regulator focusing on the safety and soundness of banks, insurers, and major investment firms, including their use of AI systems.

title: EU AI Act
description: Comprehensive European legislation regulating artificial intelligence systems based on their risk level, with specific provisions for high-risk AI in financial services.

title: EBA (European Banking Authority)
description: EU regulatory agency ensuring effective and consistent prudential regulation and supervision across European banking sector, including AI deployment guidelines.

title: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation)
description: EU regulation governing data protection and privacy, critical for AI systems that process personal data in financial services.

title: Model Validation
description: Independent review process to ensure AI models are conceptually sound, properly implemented, and perform as intended for their specific use case.

title: Backtesting
description: Testing a model's performance using historical data to assess its predictive accuracy and reliability before deployment.

title: Stress Testing
description: Evaluating model performance under extreme or adverse conditions to ensure resilience and identify potential failure points.

title: Algorithmic Bias
description: Systematic and unfair discrimination in AI system outputs, often resulting from biased training data or flawed model design.

title: Fairness in AI
description: Ensuring AI systems treat all individuals and groups equitably, without discrimination based on protected characteristics.

title: AI Transparency
description: The degree to which AI system operations, decisions, and logic are open and understandable to stakeholders and regulators.

title: Responsible AI
description: Development and deployment of AI systems that are ethical, fair, transparent, accountable, and aligned with societal values.

title: Machine Learning (ML)
description: Subset of AI where systems learn from data to improve performance on specific tasks without explicit programming.

title: Deep Learning
description: Advanced ML technique using neural networks with multiple layers to learn complex patterns in large datasets.

title: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
description: AI technology enabling computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language for applications like chatbots and document analysis.

title: Neural Network
description: Computing system inspired by biological neural networks, consisting of interconnected nodes that process and transmit information.

title: Training Data
description: Dataset used to teach AI models to recognize patterns and make predictions, critical for model accuracy and fairness.

title: Model Drift
description: Degradation in model performance over time due to changes in data patterns or relationships between variables.

title: Operational Risk
description: Risk of loss from inadequate or failed internal processes, people, systems, or external events, including AI system failures.

title: Reputational Risk
description: Potential damage to an organization's reputation from AI system failures, bias, or unethical use.

title: Third-Party Risk
description: Risks arising from reliance on external vendors for AI solutions, data, or infrastructure.

title: Cybersecurity Risk
description: Threats to AI systems from cyberattacks, data breaches, or adversarial manipulation of models.

title: MLOps (Machine Learning Operations)
description: Practices and tools for deploying, monitoring, and maintaining machine learning models in production environments.

title: Model Registry
description: Central repository for storing, versioning, and managing ML models throughout their lifecycle.

title: Model Monitoring
description: Ongoing surveillance of deployed models to detect performance degradation, data drift, and ensure continued effectiveness.

title: A/B Testing
description: Experimental method comparing two versions of a model or system to determine which performs better.

title: Champion-Challenger
description: Framework where the current production model (champion) is continuously compared against alternative models (challengers).

title: Model Versioning
description: Practice of tracking and managing different iterations of AI models to enable rollback and audit trails.

title: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)
description: Deep learning architecture specialized for processing grid-like data such as images, using convolutional layers to detect patterns.

title: Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)
description: Neural network architecture designed for sequential data processing, maintaining internal state to process sequences of inputs.

title: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
description: Special type of RNN capable of learning long-term dependencies, addressing the vanishing gradient problem.

title: Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)
description: Deep learning framework where two neural networks compete: a generator creates synthetic data and a discriminator evaluates authenticity.

title: Transformer
description: Neural network architecture using self-attention mechanisms, foundational for modern language models like GPT and BERT.

title: Attention Mechanism
description: Technique allowing models to focus on specific parts of input when producing output, crucial for sequence-to-sequence tasks.

title: Residual Network (ResNet)
description: Deep neural network architecture using skip connections to enable training of very deep networks without degradation.

title: Autoencoder
description: Neural network trained to compress data into a lower-dimensional representation and reconstruct it, useful for dimensionality reduction.

title: Variational Autoencoder (VAE)
description: Generative model that learns a probabilistic latent representation of data, enabling generation of new similar samples.

title: Graph Neural Network (GNN)
description: Neural network architecture designed to process graph-structured data, learning from node features and graph topology.

title: Capsule Network
description: Neural network architecture using capsules to better capture hierarchical relationships and spatial information in data.

title: Siamese Network
description: Neural network architecture with twin networks sharing weights, used for learning similarity metrics between inputs.

title: U-Net
description: CNN architecture for image segmentation with encoder-decoder structure and skip connections, widely used in medical imaging.

title: Inception Network
description: Deep learning architecture using inception modules with multiple filter sizes to capture features at different scales.

title: MobileNet
description: Efficient neural network architecture optimized for mobile and embedded vision applications using depthwise separable convolutions.

title: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer)
description: Family of large language models developed by OpenAI using transformer architecture for text generation and understanding.

title: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers)
description: Pre-trained language model by Google using bidirectional context to understand word meanings and relationships.

title: LLaMA (Large Language Model Meta AI)
description: Open-source large language model family by Meta designed for research and commercial applications.

title: Claude
description: Large language model developed by Anthropic focusing on safety, helpfulness, and harmlessness in AI interactions.

title: PaLM (Pathways Language Model)
description: Large language model by Google demonstrating breakthrough performance on reasoning and multilingual tasks.

title: T5 (Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer)
description: Google's unified framework treating all NLP tasks as text-to-text problems for consistent model architecture.

title: RoBERTa
description: Robustly optimized BERT approach with improved training methodology for enhanced language understanding.

title: ELECTRA
description: Efficient pre-training method for language models using replaced token detection instead of masked language modeling.

title: GPT-Neo
description: Open-source alternative to GPT models developed by EleutherAI for accessible large-scale language modeling.

title: Gemini
description: Google's multimodal AI model capable of understanding and generating text, images, audio, and video.

title: InstructGPT
description: GPT model fine-tuned using reinforcement learning from human feedback to better follow user instructions.

title: ChatGPT
description: Conversational AI model by OpenAI optimized for dialogue and interactive assistance using GPT architecture.

title: Mistral
description: High-performance open-source language model optimized for efficiency and reasoning capabilities.

title: Falcon
description: Open-source large language model developed by Technology Innovation Institute with strong multilingual capabilities.

title: MPT (MosaicML Pretrained Transformer)
description: Open-source commercially-usable language models optimized for long context and efficient training.

title: Object Detection
description: Computer vision task of identifying and locating objects within images or video streams.

title: YOLO (You Only Look Once)
description: Real-time object detection system that frames detection as a regression problem for fast inference.

title: R-CNN (Region-based CNN)
description: Object detection approach using region proposals and CNNs for accurate object localization and classification.

title: Semantic Segmentation
description: Computer vision task of classifying each pixel in an image into predefined categories.

title: Instance Segmentation
description: Computer vision task combining object detection and semantic segmentation to identify individual object instances.

title: Image Classification
description: Task of assigning predefined labels to images based on their visual content.

title: Face Recognition
description: Technology identifying or verifying individuals from digital images or video frames using facial features.

title: Optical Character Recognition (OCR)
description: Technology converting images of text into machine-encoded text for digital processing.

title: Image Generation
description: AI task of creating new images from textual descriptions, random noise, or other images.

title: Style Transfer
description: Technique applying artistic style of one image to the content of another using neural networks.

title: Image Super-Resolution
description: Enhancing image resolution and quality using deep learning to generate high-resolution images from low-resolution inputs.

title: Pose Estimation
description: Computer vision technique detecting human body positions and joint locations in images or videos.

title: Video Analysis
description: Automated processing and understanding of video content including action recognition and tracking.

title: 3D Reconstruction
description: Process of creating 3D models from 2D images or video using computer vision techniques.

title: Depth Estimation
description: Computer vision task of predicting distance of objects from camera using monocular or stereo images.

title: Q-Learning
description: Model-free reinforcement learning algorithm learning optimal action-value function to maximize cumulative reward.

title: Deep Q-Network (DQN)
description: Reinforcement learning combining Q-learning with deep neural networks for high-dimensional state spaces.

title: Policy Gradient
description: RL method directly optimizing policy parameters to maximize expected cumulative reward.

title: Actor-Critic
description: RL architecture combining value-based and policy-based methods for stable and efficient learning.

title: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)
description: Policy gradient method with clipped objective for stable and sample-efficient reinforcement learning.

title: SARSA (State-Action-Reward-State-Action)
description: On-policy reinforcement learning algorithm for learning action-value function from experience.

title: Monte Carlo Methods
description: RL techniques using complete episode samples to estimate value functions and improve policies.

title: Temporal Difference Learning
description: RL approach combining ideas from Monte Carlo and dynamic programming for online learning.

title: Multi-Armed Bandit
description: Sequential decision problem balancing exploration and exploitation with multiple action choices.

title: Markov Decision Process (MDP)
description: Mathematical framework for modeling decision-making where outcomes are partly random and partly controllable.

title: Reward Shaping
description: Technique of modifying reward signal to accelerate learning and guide agent toward desired behavior.

title: Exploration vs Exploitation
description: Fundamental tradeoff in RL between trying new actions and using known rewarding actions.

title: Model-Based RL
description: Reinforcement learning approach learning environment model to plan and predict future states.

title: Imitation Learning
description: Learning policies by observing and mimicking expert demonstrations rather than trial and error.

title: Inverse Reinforcement Learning
description: Learning reward function from observed expert behavior to understand underlying objectives.

title: Transfer Learning
description: Technique of using pre-trained model knowledge from one task to improve learning on related tasks.

title: Fine-Tuning
description: Adapting pre-trained models to specific tasks by continuing training on task-specific data.

title: Data Augmentation
description: Artificially expanding training dataset through transformations to improve model generalization.

title: Regularization
description: Techniques preventing overfitting by constraining model complexity or adding penalties to loss function.

title: Dropout
description: Regularization technique randomly dropping units during training to prevent overfitting and improve generalization.

title: Batch Normalization
description: Technique normalizing layer inputs to stabilize and accelerate neural network training.

title: Early Stopping
description: Regularization method halting training when validation performance stops improving to prevent overfitting.

title: Learning Rate Scheduling
description: Adjusting learning rate during training to improve convergence and final model performance.

title: Gradient Clipping
description: Technique limiting gradient magnitude to prevent exploding gradients during training.

title: Weight Initialization
description: Methods for setting initial neural network weights to facilitate effective training.

title: Curriculum Learning
description: Training strategy presenting examples in meaningful order from simple to complex.

title: Meta-Learning
description: Learning to learn approach where models adapt quickly to new tasks with minimal data.

title: Few-Shot Learning
description: Machine learning approach enabling models to learn from very limited labeled examples.

title: Zero-Shot Learning
description: Ability to recognize or perform tasks without any training examples through knowledge transfer.

title: Self-Supervised Learning
description: Learning representations from unlabeled data by creating pretext tasks from data itself.

title: Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD)
description: Optimization algorithm updating model parameters using gradients from random data samples.

title: Adam Optimizer
description: Adaptive learning rate optimization algorithm combining momentum and RMSprop for efficient training.

title: RMSprop
description: Optimization algorithm using adaptive learning rates based on recent gradient magnitudes.

title: AdaGrad
description: Adaptive gradient algorithm adjusting learning rate based on historical gradient information.

title: Momentum
description: Optimization technique accelerating gradient descent by accumulating velocity in consistent directions.

title: Nesterov Momentum
description: Improved momentum method using look-ahead gradient for faster convergence.

title: AdamW
description: Adam variant with decoupled weight decay for improved regularization and generalization.

title: LAMB Optimizer
description: Layer-wise adaptive learning rate optimizer for large batch training of deep neural networks.

title: RAdam
description: Rectified Adam optimizer with variance warmup for stable early training.

title: Lookahead Optimizer
description: Wrapper optimizer maintaining slow and fast weights for improved training stability.

title: Cross-Validation
description: Statistical method assessing model generalization by partitioning data and rotating training and test sets.

title: Confusion Matrix
description: Table visualizing classification model performance showing true and false predictions for each class.

title: Precision
description: Metric measuring proportion of true positive predictions among all positive predictions.

title: Recall
description: Metric measuring proportion of true positives correctly identified among all actual positives.

title: F1 Score
description: Harmonic mean of precision and recall providing balanced measure of model performance.

title: ROC Curve
description: Receiver Operating Characteristic curve plotting true positive rate vs false positive rate.

title: AUC (Area Under Curve)
description: Metric measuring area under ROC curve indicating classifier's ability to distinguish classes.

title: Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
description: Average absolute difference between predicted and actual values in regression tasks.

title: Mean Squared Error (MSE)
description: Average squared difference between predicted and actual values, penalizing larger errors more heavily.

title: R-Squared
description: Statistical measure indicating proportion of variance in dependent variable explained by model.

title: Perplexity
description: Metric evaluating language model performance based on how well it predicts sample text.

title: BLEU Score
description: Metric for evaluating machine translation quality by comparing to reference translations.

title: Intersection over Union (IoU)
description: Metric measuring overlap between predicted and ground truth regions in object detection.

title: Log Loss
description: Loss function measuring performance of classification model outputting probability values.

title: Accuracy
description: Proportion of correct predictions among total predictions made by classifier.

title: Feature Selection
description: Process of identifying and selecting most relevant features for model training.

title: Feature Extraction
description: Creating new features from raw data to improve model performance and interpretability.

title: Dimensionality Reduction
description: Reducing number of features while retaining important information using techniques like PCA.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
description: Technique transforming correlated features into uncorrelated principal components.

title: t-SNE
description: Dimensionality reduction technique for visualizing high-dimensional data in 2D or 3D space.

title: UMAP
description: Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection for dimensionality reduction and visualization.

title: One-Hot Encoding
description: Converting categorical variables into binary vector representation for machine learning.

title: Label Encoding
description: Converting categorical labels into numerical format for algorithm processing.

title: Normalization
description: Scaling features to standard range to improve model training and convergence.

title: Standardization
description: Transforming features to have zero mean and unit variance for better model performance.

title: Binning
description: Grouping continuous values into discrete bins or categories for simplified modeling.

title: Feature Hashing
description: Encoding categorical features using hash function for memory-efficient representation.

title: Polynomial Features
description: Creating interaction and polynomial terms from existing features for non-linear modeling.

title: Embedding
description: Dense vector representation of discrete variables capturing semantic relationships.

title: TF-IDF
description: Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency for representing text importance in documents.

title: Decision Tree
description: Tree-structured model making predictions through hierarchical decision rules.

title: Random Forest
description: Ensemble method combining multiple decision trees for improved accuracy and robustness.

title: Support Vector Machine (SVM)
description: Supervised learning algorithm finding optimal hyperplane for classification or regression.

title: K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)
description: Instance-based learning algorithm classifying based on majority vote of k nearest examples.

title: Naive Bayes
description: Probabilistic classifier based on Bayes' theorem with independence assumptions.

title: Logistic Regression
description: Statistical model for binary classification using logistic function.

title: Linear Regression
description: Statistical method modeling relationship between dependent and independent variables.

title: Gradient Boosting
description: Ensemble technique building models sequentially to correct predecessor errors.

title: XGBoost
description: Optimized gradient boosting library with regularization for high performance.

title: LightGBM
description: Gradient boosting framework using tree-based learning for efficiency and speed.

title: CatBoost
description: Gradient boosting library handling categorical features automatically.

title: AdaBoost
description: Adaptive boosting algorithm combining weak learners into strong classifier.

title: K-Means Clustering
description: Unsupervised algorithm partitioning data into k clusters based on similarity.

title: DBSCAN
description: Density-based clustering algorithm identifying clusters of arbitrary shape.

title: Hierarchical Clustering
description: Clustering method building tree of clusters through agglomerative or divisive approach.

title: Diffusion Model
description: Generative model learning to reverse gradual noise addition process for image and data generation.

title: Stable Diffusion
description: Latent diffusion model for text-to-image generation with high quality and efficiency.

title: DALL-E
description: OpenAI's text-to-image generation model creating images from textual descriptions.

title: Midjourney
description: AI image generation service creating artistic images from text prompts.

title: Text-to-Image Generation
description: AI task creating visual content from natural language descriptions.

title: Text-to-Video Generation
description: AI capability generating video content from textual descriptions or scripts.

title: ControlNet
description: Neural network architecture adding spatial conditioning to diffusion models for controlled generation.

title: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
description: Efficient fine-tuning technique adapting large models with minimal additional parameters.

title: Prompt Engineering
description: Crafting effective input prompts to guide AI models toward desired outputs.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
description: Prompting technique encouraging models to show reasoning steps for complex problems.

title: In-Context Learning
description: Large language model capability to learn from examples provided in prompt without parameter updates.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
description: Technique enhancing LLM responses by retrieving relevant information from external knowledge bases.

title: Vector Database
description: Specialized database storing and querying high-dimensional vector embeddings for similarity search.

title: Hallucination
description: Phenomenon where AI models generate plausible but factually incorrect information.

title: Temperature Sampling
description: Controlling randomness in model outputs by adjusting probability distribution temperature.

title: TensorFlow
description: Open-source machine learning framework by Google for building and deploying ML models.

title: PyTorch
description: Open-source machine learning library by Meta for deep learning research and production.

title: Keras
description: High-level neural networks API running on top of TensorFlow for rapid prototyping.

title: Scikit-learn
description: Python library for classical machine learning algorithms and data preprocessing.

title: Hugging Face
description: Platform and library for state-of-the-art NLP models and pre-trained transformers.

title: LangChain
description: Framework for developing applications powered by language models with composable components.

title: JAX
description: Google's library for high-performance numerical computing and machine learning research.

title: Apache Spark
description: Unified analytics engine for large-scale data processing and machine learning.

title: MLflow
description: Open-source platform for managing ML lifecycle including experimentation and deployment.

title: Weights & Biases
description: Platform for experiment tracking, model visualization, and collaboration in ML projects.

title: TensorBoard
description: Visualization toolkit for TensorFlow providing metrics, graphs, and model debugging.

title: ONNX
description: Open Neural Network Exchange format for model interoperability across frameworks.

title: Docker
description: Platform for containerizing applications ensuring consistent deployment across environments.

title: Kubernetes
description: Container orchestration platform for automating deployment and scaling of applications.

title: Ray
description: Framework for scaling Python and ML applications from laptop to cluster.

title: ETL (Extract, Transform, Load)
description: Data pipeline process extracting data from sources, transforming it, and loading into destination.

title: Data Pipeline
description: Automated workflow moving data from source to destination with transformations.

title: Data Lake
description: Centralized repository storing structured and unstructured data at any scale.

title: Data Warehouse
description: Central repository of integrated data from multiple sources for analysis and reporting.

title: Apache Kafka
description: Distributed event streaming platform for high-throughput real-time data pipelines.

title: Apache Airflow
description: Platform for programmatically authoring, scheduling, and monitoring workflows.

title: Pandas
description: Python library providing data structures and analysis tools for structured data.

title: NumPy
description: Fundamental package for numerical computing in Python with array operations.

title: Dask
description: Parallel computing library scaling Python workflows to multi-core machines and clusters.

title: Apache Hadoop
description: Framework for distributed storage and processing of large datasets.

title: Data Cleaning
description: Process of detecting and correcting inaccuracies and inconsistencies in datasets.

title: Missing Data Imputation
description: Techniques for filling in missing values in datasets using statistical methods.

title: Outlier Detection
description: Identifying data points significantly different from other observations.

title: Data Versioning
description: Tracking changes to datasets over time for reproducibility and collaboration.

title: Synthetic Data Generation
description: Creating artificial data that mimics real data characteristics for training and testing.

title: Edge Computing
description: Processing data near source rather than centralized cloud for reduced latency.

title: TensorFlow Lite
description: Lightweight solution for deploying ML models on mobile and embedded devices.

title: Core ML
description: Apple's framework for integrating machine learning models into iOS applications.

title: ONNX Runtime
description: Cross-platform engine for running ONNX models with optimized performance.

title: Model Quantization
description: Reducing model size and inference time by using lower precision representations.

title: Model Pruning
description: Removing unnecessary weights and connections from neural networks to reduce size.

title: Knowledge Distillation
description: Training smaller student model to mimic larger teacher model's behavior.

title: Neural Architecture Search (NAS)
description: Automated process of finding optimal neural network architecture for specific tasks.

title: Federated Learning
description: Training models across decentralized devices without exchanging raw data.

title: On-Device AI
description: Running machine learning inference directly on edge devices without cloud connectivity.

title: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR)
description: Converting spoken language into text using machine learning models.

title: Text-to-Speech (TTS)
description: Synthesizing natural-sounding speech from written text.

title: Whisper
description: OpenAI's robust speech recognition model trained on diverse audio data.

title: Speaker Recognition
description: Identifying or verifying individuals based on voice characteristics.

title: Audio Classification
description: Categorizing audio signals into predefined classes or categories.

title: Music Generation
description: Creating original musical compositions using AI models.

title: Sound Event Detection
description: Identifying and classifying sound events in audio recordings.

title: Voice Cloning
description: Replicating person's voice characteristics using small audio samples.

title: Mel Spectrogram
description: Visual representation of audio spectrum on mel scale for audio processing.

title: WaveNet
description: DeepMind's generative model for raw audio waveform synthesis.

title: SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping)
description: Building map of environment while tracking robot's location within it.

title: Motion Planning
description: Computing path for robot to move from start to goal avoiding obstacles.

title: ROS (Robot Operating System)
description: Flexible framework for writing robot software with libraries and tools.

title: Computer Vision for Robotics
description: Enabling robots to interpret and understand visual world for navigation and manipulation.

title: Autonomous Navigation
description: Self-directed movement of robots through environment without human intervention.

title: Object Manipulation
description: Robotic grasping, picking, and placing objects using vision and planning.

title: Sensor Fusion
description: Combining data from multiple sensors for more accurate perception.

title: Path Planning
description: Finding optimal route between positions considering constraints and objectives.

title: Swarm Intelligence
description: Collective behavior of decentralized systems inspired by nature.

title: Digital Twin
description: Virtual replica of physical system for simulation, monitoring, and optimization.

title: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations)
description: Game-theoretic approach explaining model predictions by computing feature contributions.

title: LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations)
description: Explaining individual predictions by approximating model locally with interpretable model.

title: Feature Importance
description: Quantifying contribution of each feature to model's predictions.

title: Attention Visualization
description: Displaying which input parts model focuses on when making predictions.

title: Saliency Maps
description: Highlighting image regions most influential for neural network's decision.

title: Grad-CAM
description: Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping for visualizing CNN decisions.

title: Counterfactual Explanations
description: Explaining predictions by identifying minimal changes for different outcome.

title: Model Cards
description: Documentation providing transparency about ML model characteristics and limitations.

title: Anchors
description: Explanation method finding sufficient conditions for predictions to hold.

title: Partial Dependence Plots
description: Visualizing marginal effect of features on predicted outcome.

title: AI Alignment
description: Ensuring AI systems pursue intended goals and values aligned with human interests.

title: Constitutional AI
description: Training AI to be helpful, harmless, and honest through constitutional principles.

title: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
description: Fine-tuning models using human preferences to improve alignment and helpfulness.

title: Red Teaming
description: Testing AI systems by simulating adversarial attacks to identify vulnerabilities.

title: Adversarial Examples
description: Inputs deliberately designed to cause model to make mistakes.

title: Model Robustness
description: AI system's ability to maintain performance under distributional shifts and attacks.

title: Safety Constraints
description: Rules and limitations ensuring AI systems operate within acceptable boundaries.

title: Value Learning
description: AI systems learning human values and preferences from observations and feedback.

title: AI Sandboxing
description: Isolating AI systems in controlled environments to prevent unintended consequences.

title: Mechanistic Interpretability
description: Understanding internal mechanisms of neural networks at computational level.

title: Multimodal Learning
description: Training models to process and relate information from multiple data modalities.

title: CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training)
description: OpenAI model learning visual concepts from natural language descriptions.

title: Vision-Language Models
description: AI systems understanding relationships between visual and textual information.

title: Cross-Modal Retrieval
description: Searching across different modalities using query from another modality.

title: Image Captioning
description: Automatically generating textual descriptions for images.

title: Visual Question Answering (VQA)
description: Answering questions about image content using vision and language understanding.

title: Audio-Visual Learning
description: Learning from synchronized audio and visual inputs for enhanced understanding.

title: Video Understanding
description: Analyzing and interpreting video content including actions, events, and temporal relationships.

title: Embodied AI
description: AI agents that learn through interaction with physical or simulated environments.

title: Scene Understanding
description: Comprehensive interpretation of visual scenes including objects, relationships, and context.

title: Time Series Forecasting
description: Predicting future values based on historical temporal data patterns.

title: ARIMA
description: AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average model for time series analysis.

title: Prophet
description: Facebook's forecasting tool for time series with strong seasonal patterns.

title: Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN)
description: CNN architecture for sequence modeling with causal convolutions.

title: Anomaly Detection
description: Identifying unusual patterns or outliers in data that deviate from normal behavior.

title: Seasonal Decomposition
description: Separating time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components.

title: Autocorrelation
description: Correlation of time series with lagged version of itself.

title: Stationarity
description: Time series property where statistical properties remain constant over time.

title: Exponential Smoothing
description: Forecasting method using weighted averages of past observations with exponentially decreasing weights.

title: Wavelet Transform
description: Mathematical technique for analyzing signals at different frequencies and time scales.

title: Collaborative Filtering
description: Recommending items based on similar users' preferences and behaviors.

title: Content-Based Filtering
description: Recommending items similar to those user previously liked based on features.

title: Matrix Factorization
description: Decomposing user-item interaction matrix for latent feature discovery.

title: Neural Collaborative Filtering
description: Using deep learning for collaborative filtering in recommendation systems.

title: Hybrid Recommender Systems
description: Combining multiple recommendation techniques for improved performance.

title: Cold Start Problem
description: Challenge of recommending to new users or items with insufficient interaction data.

title: Implicit Feedback
description: Inferring user preferences from indirect signals like clicks and views.

title: Click-Through Rate (CTR) Prediction
description: Estimating probability of user clicking on recommended item or ad.

title: Session-Based Recommendations
description: Making recommendations based on current user session without historical data.

title: Diversity in Recommendations
description: Ensuring variety in recommended items to avoid filter bubbles.

title: Knowledge Graph
description: Structured representation of entities and relationships enabling semantic reasoning.

title: Ontology
description: Formal representation of knowledge domain including concepts and relationships.

title: Semantic Web
description: Extension of web enabling machines to understand and process content meaningfully.

title: Entity Recognition
description: Identifying and classifying named entities in text into predefined categories.

title: Relation Extraction
description: Identifying semantic relationships between entities in text.

title: Triple Store
description: Database optimized for storing and querying RDF triples in knowledge graphs.

title: SPARQL
description: Query language for retrieving and manipulating data stored in RDF format.

title: Schema Mapping
description: Defining correspondences between different data schemas for integration.

title: Semantic Similarity
description: Measuring relatedness between concepts based on meaning rather than syntax.

title: Knowledge Base
description: Centralized repository storing structured and unstructured information.

title: AutoML (Automated Machine Learning)
description: Automating end-to-end machine learning pipeline from data to deployed model.

title: H2O AutoML
description: Automated machine learning platform for scalable model training and tuning.

title: AutoKeras
description: AutoML library based on Keras for automated neural architecture search.

title: TPOT
description: Tree-based Pipeline Optimization Tool using genetic programming for AutoML.

title: Hyperparameter Optimization
description: Systematically searching for optimal hyperparameter configurations.

title: Grid Search
description: Exhaustively searching through manually specified hyperparameter combinations.

title: Random Search
description: Randomly sampling hyperparameter combinations from specified distributions.

title: Bayesian Optimization
description: Probabilistic approach to hyperparameter tuning using surrogate models.

title: Optuna
description: Hyperparameter optimization framework with automatic search algorithm selection.

description: Model architecture with specialized sub-models activated conditionally.
